references.title: Student’s performance prediction based on an improved multi-view hypergraph neural network
references.journal: Journal of Experimental & Theoretical Artificial Intelligence

extracted.Students: 3563 students from an University in Zhejiang
extracted.Data sources: Course Performance History, Once-Card Consumption, Library Usage, Cellphone Use Time, Vacation Information
extracted.Features: [Student Information, Breakfast Behavior, Lunch Behavior, Dinner Behavior, Shopping Behavior, Library Behavior, No-Cellphone Behavior, Vacation Behavior]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors combine data from multiple sources to create a dataset. For each one of hte features, the authors extract multiple features: Frequency, Shannon entropy, Average Time, Standard Deviation, Mode, Min, Max, Q1, Q3, Median, Latest, Location, etc…

Authors select eatures using ANOVA and Pearson Correlation. Additionally, the class imbalance is addressed using oversampling: SMOTE, Borderline-SMOTE and class_weights.

The data is combined to create a multiview hypergraph meant to find similarities (differences) between learners - using an adjacency matirx.
extracted.Models: SVM, kNN, Decision Tree, MLP, GCN, GTN, HGNN, IMHNN
extracted.Comments:
Authors create a predictive model of GPA prediction that involves the creation of hypergraphs.

Authors collect data from the first 5 months and create multiple datasets for multiple moments of prediction. The model, which uses the hypergraph as input to a neural network outperforms all other baselines used on all datasets.

The authors perform an ablation study on the different elements of the network. Accuracy is higher (but not that much) in comparison with ResNet and Egat_Conv.
extracted.Limitations:
- No temporal representation

- Does not account for important predictors related with learner characteristics.

- Data used refers to a very specific context

- Very specific to a university that has the means to extract (and use) a lot of sensitive information.

annotations.text: Current models to predict academic performance focus on attributes of the students but not the relationships between the students themselves. Authors propose a multi-view Hypergraph neural network to address this issue:
Comparison of this model against other models ion the task of predicting student performance shows that the model outperforms baseline.
